title: LongMemEval-V2: Evaluating Long-Term Agent Memory Toward Experienced Colleagues
core_claim: The paper introduces LongMemEval-V2, a benchmark for testing whether long-term memory systems help web agents retain environment-specific experience, and proposes two memory methods with one achieving the best accuracy-latency tradeoff in their experiments.
triage_rationale: This looks like a solid benchmark-and-systems paper with a clear problem, concrete evaluation setup, and measurable results, which makes it useful for review. It is less likely to be a breakthrough ML paper than a strong applied infrastructure/agent-memory contribution, so it belongs in review rather than an immediate advance.
likely_red_flags: Benchmark-centric contribution rather than a new general learning method
Performance claims are tied to a specific curated setup and may not generalize
Coding-agent-based memory method may have high latency and operational complexity
